one_liner: NVDA +1.3% to $195 — RSI 33.3 is oversold territory and DCF shows 41.6% upside, but the stock is 9% below the 50-day EMA with no Wyckoff accumulation signal yet.

price_action.intraday_story: NVDA opened at $193.85, dipped to $189.80 (near its recent multi-week low), then recovered to close at $194.97. Volume at 146.6M (0.87x avg) was below average — consistent with a low-conviction bounce rather than an institutional accumulation event.

news_catalyst.headline: AI Chip Rally Extends as Cantor Says Chip Upcycle Is Far from Over
news_catalyst.summary: NVDA is participating in the broad tech recovery today. Cantor noted the AI chip demand cycle has significant runway remaining, and the broader market tech rally (SPY +1.64%) is lifting semiconductor names. NVDA's 13% drawdown from its recent high reflects concerns about supply chain and competition but analysts remain broadly bullish. AWS GPU price hikes (Amazon news) signal data center operators are still constrained — a structural positive for Nvidia.

technical.summary: RSI at 33.3 is approaching oversold territory (<30) for the first time since the current drawdown began. Stock sits between EMA50 ($213.77) and EMA200 ($220.91) — both well overhead. Lower BB at $189.56 is being tested. Volume drying up below average is the first positive sign for a base-building thesis, but no Wyckoff accumulation signals have fired. DCF at $333.93 (41.6% upside) provides a fundamental anchor.

wyckoff_narrative: No formal Wyckoff events detected. The low-volume consolidation near the lower Bollinger Band could be the early stages of Phase A (Stopping the Downtrend), but a climactic volume event is needed to confirm the SC. Watch for a high-volume flush below $190 followed by a V-reversal as the potential SC setup.

valuation.summary: DCF base case at $333.93 represents 41.6% upside — one of the larger margins of safety in the watchlist for a profitable, growing business. Grade A fundamentals with 82% earnings growth support the DCF inputs.

key_strengths: Dominant AI training and inference GPU market share
Data center revenue continuing to grow >100% YoY
Grade A fundamentals score 0.82 — best-in-class profitability
key_concerns: 13% drawdown suggests institutional distribution — no confirmed bottom
Chinese AI competition (DeepSeek, Huawei) challenging inference market share
Supply constraints from TSMC could limit upside in near term
fundamentals.summary: Grade A business at a reasonable valuation after the drawdown. The stock is watch, not accumulate, because no technical bottom has been confirmed.

risks: No Wyckoff SC event confirmed — could see further drawdown to $175–$180 before a proper bottom forms
EMA50 at $213.77 is 9.6% overhead — full trend reversal requires significant work
Chinese competition in inference chips accelerating — long-term market share at risk
